heading: Required payments for entities electing not to have required taxable year
label: 7519 Required payments for entities electing not to have required taxable year

children.text: an election under section 444 is in effect for the taxable year, and
the required payment determined under subsection (b) for such taxable year (or any preceding taxable year) exceeds $500.

children.label: A
children.text: the applicable percentage of the adjusted highest section 1 rate, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the net base year income of the entity, over
the net required payment balance.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: If, for any applicable election year, the amount determined under subsection (b)(2) exceeds the amount determined under subsection (b)(1), the entity shall be entitled to a refund of such excess for such year.

children.label: A
children.text: an election under section 444 is terminated effective with respect to any year, or

children.label: B
children.text: the entity is liquidated during any year, the entity shall be entitled to a refund of the net required payment balance.

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of the year referred to in paragraph (1), the calendar year in which it begins,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of the year referred to in paragraph (2), the calendar year in which it ends, or

children.label: B
children.text: the day 90 days after the day on which claim therefor is filed with the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the deferral ratio multiplied by the entity’s net income for the base year, plus

children.label: i
children.text: the deferral ratio multiplied by the aggregate amount of applicable payments made by the entity during the base year, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate amount of such applicable payments made during the deferral period of the base year.

children.heading: Partnerships
children.label: A Partnerships
children.text: In the case of a partnership, net income shall be the amount (not below zero) determined by taking into account the aggregate amount of the partnership’s items described in section 702(a) (other than credits and tax-exempt income).

children.heading: S corporations
children.label: B S corporations
children.text: In the case of an S corporation, net income shall be the amount (not below zero) determined by taking into account the aggregate amount of the S corporation’s items described in section 1366(a) (other than credits and tax-exempt income). If the S corporation was a C corporation for the base year, its taxable income for such year shall be treated as its net income for such year (and such corporation shall be treated as an S corporation for such taxable year for purposes of paragraph (3)).

children.heading: Certain limitations disregarded
children.label: C Certain limitations disregarded
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A) or (B), any limitation on the amount of any item described in either such paragraph which may be taken into account for purposes of computing the taxable income of a partner or shareholder shall be disregarded.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “applicable payment” means amounts paid by a partnership or S corporation which are includible in gross income of a partner or shareholder.

children.label: i
children.text: gain from the sale or exchange of property between the partner or shareholder and the partnership or S corporation, and

children.label: ii
children.text: dividend paid by the S corporation.

children.heading: Applicable percentage
children.label: 4 Applicable percentage
children.text: The applicable percentage is the percentage determined in accordance with the following table:If the applicable election year ofthe partnership or S corporationbegins during:The applicable percentage is:1987251988501989751990 or thereafter100.Notwithstanding the preceding provisions of this paragraph, the applicable percentage for any partnership or S corporation shall be 100 percent unless more than 50 percent of such entity’s net income for the short taxable year which would have resulted if the entity had not made an election under section 444 would have been allocated to partners or shareholders who would have been entitled to the benefits of section 806(e)(2)(C) of the Tax Reform Act of 1986 with respect to such income.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any guaranteed payment by a partnership shall not be treated as an applicable payment, and the amount of the net income of the partnership shall be determined by not taking such guaranteed payment into account.

children.heading: Guaranteed payment
children.label: B Guaranteed payment
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “guaranteed payment” means any payment referred to in section 707(c).

children.heading: Other definitions and special rules
children.label: e Other definitions and special rules

children.heading: Deferral period
children.label: 1 Deferral period
children.text: The term “deferral period” has the meaning given to such term by section 444(b)(4).

children.heading: Base year
children.label: A Base year
children.text: The term “base year” means, with respect to any applicable election year, the taxable year of the partnership or S corporation preceding such applicable election year.

children.heading: Applicable election year
children.label: B Applicable election year
children.text: The term “applicable election year” means any taxable year of a partnership or S corporation with respect to which an election is in effect under section 444.

children.heading: Requirement of reporting
children.label: 3 Requirement of reporting
children.text: Each partnership or S corporation which makes an election under section 444 shall include on any required return or statement such information as the Secretary shall prescribe as is necessary to carry out the provisions of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate of the required payments under this section for all preceding applicable election years, over

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount allowable as a refund to the entity under subsection (c) for all preceding applicable election years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subsection or in regulations prescribed by the Secretary, any payment required by this section shall be assessed and collected in the same manner as if it were a tax imposed by subtitle C.

children.heading: Due date
children.label: 2 Due date
children.text: The amount of any payment required by this section shall be paid on or before April 15 of the calendar year following the calendar year in which the applicable election year begins (or such later date as may be prescribed by the Secretary).

children.heading: Interest
children.label: 3 Interest
children.text: For purposes of determining interest, any payment required by this section shall be treated as a tax; except that no interest shall be allowed with respect to any refund of a payment made under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In the case of any failure by any person to pay on the date prescribed therefor any amount required by this section, there shall be imposed on such person a penalty of 10 percent of the underpayment. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “underpayment” means the excess of the amount of the payment required under this section over the amount (if any) of such payment paid on or before the date prescribed therefor. No penalty shall be imposed under this subparagraph on any failure which is shown to be due to reasonable cause and not willful neglect.

children.heading: Negligence and fraud penalties made applicable
children.label: B Negligence and fraud penalties made applicable
children.text: For purposes of part II of subchapter A of chapter 68, any payment required by this section shall be treated as a tax.

children.heading: Willful failure
children.label: C Willful failure
children.text: If any partnership or S corporation willfully fails to comply with the requirements of this section, section 444 shall cease to apply with respect to such partnership or S corporation.
2 or more applicable election years begin in the same calendar year, or
the base year is a taxable year of less than 12 months.
